evals.prompt: The create-changelog custom skill was deleted from .claude/skills/ but is still referenced in README.md and AGENTS.md. Run the align-skills-documentation skill and verify it removes all references to create-changelog from both files. The skill should automatically detect that create-changelog SKILL.md no longer exists and clean up the documentation.
evals.expected_output: README.md and AGENTS.md updated to remove create-changelog entries. Report shows create-changelog in 'removed' section with details about what was cleaned up.

assertions.name: create_changelog_removed_from_readme_table
assertions.description: Verify create-changelog row is removed from skills table in README.md

assertions.name: create_changelog_removed_from_agents_table
assertions.description: Verify create-changelog row is removed from AGENTS.md custom skills section

assertions.name: create_changelog_not_in_directory_listing
assertions.description: Verify create-changelog is not in README.md directory structure listing

assertions.name: report_lists_removed_skill
assertions.description: Report should show create-changelog in 'removed' or 'orphaned' section with count

evals.prompt: Run the align-skills-documentation skill on a project where all custom skills exist (pr, github-mcp-setup, align-skills-documentation, semantic-release, git-branch). Verify the skill correctly discovers all existing custom skills and doesn't accidentally remove or modify valid documentation. The report should show zero removed skills.
evals.expected_output: All existing custom skills properly documented. No spurious removals. Report shows all custom skills present with accurate metadata and 'Custom' type.

assertions.name: all_custom_skills_discovered
assertions.description: All five custom skills (pr, github-mcp-setup, align-skills-documentation, semantic-release, git-branch) are discovered

assertions.name: custom_skills_marked_correctly
assertions.description: All custom skills are marked with type='Custom' not 'Open-source'

assertions.name: existing_skills_preserved
assertions.description: All existing custom skills remain in README.md skills table

assertions.name: no_false_removals
assertions.description: Report shows zero removed skills (no false positives)

evals.prompt: The project has both frontend-design and skill-creator from skills-lock.json (open-source from anthropics/skills) AND custom skills like pr and github-mcp-setup. Run the align-skills-documentation skill and verify: (1) frontend-design and skill-creator are marked as 'Open-source' type with source='anthropics/skills', (2) pr and github-mcp-setup are marked as 'Custom' type with local SKILL.md paths, (3) no skill is marked as both types. This tests that discovery correctly prioritizes skills-lock.json sources.
evals.expected_output: Skills correctly categorized: frontend-design and skill-creator as Open-source, pr and github-mcp-setup as Custom. No type conflicts or duplicate entries.

assertions.name: opensource_skills_correctly_identified
assertions.description: frontend-design and skill-creator marked as Open-source type from anthropics/skills

assertions.name: custom_skills_correctly_identified
assertions.description: pr, github-mcp-setup, semantic-release marked as Custom type with .claude/skills paths

assertions.name: no_type_conflicts
assertions.description: No skill appears with conflicting types or source attributions

assertions.name: source_attribution_correct
assertions.description: Open-source skills link to 'anthropics/skills', Custom skills link to local paths

evals.prompt: A project documentation has three non-existing skills referenced in docs: 'old-tool', 'deprecated-helper', 'removed-workflow'. Actual available skills: pr, github-mcp-setup, align-skills-documentation. Run the skill and verify: (1) removes all three non-existing skills from README and AGENTS.md, (2) discovers and documents only the three actual skills as Custom type, (3) generates detailed report showing what was cleaned up.
evals.expected_output: All three non-existing skills removed from both files. Only three actual skills documented as Custom. Clean audit report listing removed skills.

assertions.name: all_orphaned_skills_removed
assertions.description: All three non-existing skills completely removed from README and AGENTS.md

assertions.name: only_existing_skills_documented
assertions.description: Only the three actual skills appear in documentation

assertions.name: no_orphaned_references_remain
assertions.description: No lingering references to removed skills in any section (table, integration guide, directory)

assertions.name: cleanup_report_complete
assertions.description: Report accurately lists all three removed skills with what was cleaned up

evals.prompt: The project has: (1) Custom skills in .claude/skills/ (semantic-release, pr, git-branch), (2) Open-source skills in skills-lock.json (frontend-design, skill-creator), (3) Agent skills from enabled agent-skills plugin (spec, plan, build, test, review), (4) orphaned references to 'old-skill' in docs. Run align-skills-documentation and verify correct discovery and categorization of all skill types, with only one entry per skill regardless of source.
evals.expected_output: All skill types correctly discovered and categorized. No duplicate entries. Orphaned 'old-skill' removed. Report shows accurate counts: 3 Custom, 2 Open-source, 5 Agent Skills.

assertions.name: custom_skills_counted_correctly
assertions.description: Exactly 3 custom skills discovered and marked as Custom type

assertions.name: opensource_skills_counted_correctly
assertions.description: Exactly 2 open-source skills discovered and marked as Open-source type

assertions.name: agent_skills_counted_correctly
assertions.description: At least 5 agent skills discovered and marked as Agent Skill type

assertions.name: no_duplicates_across_sources
assertions.description: No skill appears multiple times in discovered skills with different types

assertions.name: orphaned_removed
assertions.description: Orphaned 'old-skill' is removed from documentation

evals.prompt: Run align-skills-documentation on the project with all skills present (5 custom, 2 open-source, 29+ agent skills from the plugin). Verify that the README.md skills table is generated with ALL discovered skills, properly sorted by type (Custom first, then Open-source, then Agent Skills). Each row should have correct: skill name, type label, truncated description, and source link matching the skill type.
evals.expected_output: README.md skills table contains all 36+ discovered skills, properly sorted by type. Each custom skill links to ./.claude/skills/{name}/SKILL.md, open-source skills link to anthropics/skills, agent skills link to addyosmani/agent-skills. Descriptions are truncated to 1-2 sentences. Table is syntactically valid markdown.

assertions.name: all_custom_skills_in_table
assertions.description: All 5 custom skills appear in README skills table

assertions.name: all_opensource_skills_in_table
assertions.description: Both open-source skills (frontend-design, skill-creator) appear with correct source attribution

assertions.name: agent_skills_populated
assertions.description: 29+ agent skills are present in the table with Agent Skill type label

assertions.name: table_sorted_by_type
assertions.description: Skills are sorted in order: Custom, then Open-source, then Agent Skills

assertions.name: source_links_correct
assertions.description: Custom skills link to local .claude/skills paths, open-source links to anthropics/skills, agent skills to addyosmani/agent-skills

assertions.name: descriptions_truncated
assertions.description: All skill descriptions are truncated to 1-2 sentences, not full descriptions

assertions.name: table_markdown_valid
assertions.description: Generated table is valid markdown with correct pipe separators and header format

evals.prompt: Run align-skills-documentation and verify that the Trigger Context & Usage Patterns section is generated in README.md. The section should appear after the skills table and categorize all 36+ skills into groups like 'Workflow Planning & Specification', 'Implementation & Build', 'Testing & Debugging', 'Code Review & Quality', 'Frontend & UI', and 'Custom Project Skills'. Each skill should have a brief description of when it should be used.
evals.expected_output: README.md has new 'Trigger Context & Usage Patterns' section with 6+ categories listing all discovered skills with usage patterns. Section appears between skills table and 'Quick Start' section. All skills are represented with trigger context.

assertions.name: trigger_context_section_exists
assertions.description: Section '### Trigger Context & Usage Patterns' exists in README.md

assertions.name: section_has_categories
assertions.description: Section contains organized categories (Workflow Planning, Implementation & Build, Testing, Code Review, Frontend & UI, Custom)

assertions.name: all_skills_included_in_context
assertions.description: All 36+ discovered skills appear somewhere in the Trigger Context section

assertions.name: custom_skills_listed
assertions.description: Custom skills (pr, semantic-release, git-branch, etc.) appear in 'Custom Project Skills' or appropriate category with trigger patterns

assertions.name: trigger_patterns_descriptive
assertions.description: Each skill has a brief description of when/why to use it (e.g., 'Use when creating feature branches...', 'Use this skill to work with...')

assertions.name: section_location_correct
assertions.description: Trigger Context section appears after skills table and before 'Quick Start' or similar sections

evals.prompt: Run align-skills-documentation and verify that AGENTS.md receives updates to four new comprehensive sections: (1) Decision Trees - if-then workflow selection for scenarios like 'I need to implement a feature' and 'Something is broken'; (2) Agent Combinations - matrix showing workflow sequences and context flow; (3) Custom Skills Integration - table showing where custom skills fit into agent workflows; (4) Agent Context Requirements - prerequisites and state for each agent. All sections should be properly formatted markdown with clear structure.
evals.expected_output: AGENTS.md has 4 new sections with proper headers (## 🎯 Decision Trees, ## 🔗 Agent Combinations & Context Flow, ## 🛠️ Custom Skills Integration Guide, ## 📋 Agent Context Requirements). All sections contain valid markdown tables or structured content. Sections appear around lines 158-275 in AGENTS.md.

assertions.name: decision_trees_section_exists
assertions.description: Section '## 🎯 Decision Trees' exists in AGENTS.md with if-then workflow selection logic

assertions.name: decision_trees_have_scenarios
assertions.description: Decision Trees include multiple scenarios: feature implementation, debugging, optimization, security, refactoring

assertions.name: agent_combinations_section_exists
assertions.description: Section '## 🔗 Agent Combinations & Context Flow' exists with workflow sequence matrix

assertions.name: combinations_show_context_flow
assertions.description: Agent Combinations matrix shows how context flows between agents (e.g., spec output → plan input)

assertions.name: custom_skills_integration_exists
assertions.description: Section '## 🛠️ Custom Skills Integration Guide' exists documenting where custom skills connect to agent workflows

assertions.name: custom_skills_mapped_correctly
assertions.description: Custom skills (pr, semantic-release, align-skills-documentation) are mapped to their integration points in agent workflows

assertions.name: context_requirements_section_exists
assertions.description: Section '## 📋 Agent Context Requirements' exists documenting prerequisites and state for major agents

assertions.name: all_sections_valid_markdown
assertions.description: All 4 new sections use valid markdown with proper tables, headers, and formatting

evals.prompt: Run align-skills-documentation on a clean project state where all 36+ skills exist and no orphaned references remain. This tests the complete synchronization workflow: skill discovery, README skills table generation, Trigger Context generation, AGENTS.md section generation, orphaned skill detection (should be zero), and comprehensive reporting. Verify the entire documentation stays in sync with available skills.
evals.expected_output: Complete documentation sync: README has all skills in table + Trigger Context section, AGENTS.md has all 4 new sections, report shows zero orphaned skills, all 36+ skills categorized correctly by type, documentation clean status reported as true.

assertions.name: all_skills_discovered_and_documented
assertions.description: All 36+ skills from all sources (custom, open-source, agent plugins) are discovered and appear in documentation

assertions.name: no_orphaned_skills_detected
assertions.description: Report shows zero orphaned skills - documentation is in sync with actual skills

assertions.name: readme_fully_updated
assertions.description: README.md has complete skills table + Trigger Context section with all skills represented

assertions.name: agents_md_fully_generated
assertions.description: AGENTS.md has all 4 new sections: Decision Trees, Agent Combinations, Custom Skills Integration, Context Requirements

assertions.name: skill_types_categorized_correctly
assertions.description: All skills correctly marked as Custom, Open-source, or Agent Skill with proper type labels and source links

assertions.name: documentation_clean_status
assertions.description: Report indicates 'documentation_clean: true' showing perfect sync between actual and documented skills

assertions.name: report_comprehensive
assertions.description: Report shows skill counts by type, discovery sources, and overall synchronization status
